etymology.description: semantic 辵(辵) (chuò, "walk; movement") + phonetic 咼(咼) (guō) -> exceed; pass through
etymology.interpretation: This character combines the semantic element 辵(辵) (chuò, "walk; movement"), indicating movement or transit, with the phonetic element 咼(咼) (guō), which suggests the sound. The concept of movement suggests the idea of passing or exceeding a certain point.
etymology.simplification: 过 is simplified by replacing 辵(辵) (chuò, "walk; movement") with 辶, a simplified version of the radical indicating movement.

examples.chinese: 我已经吃过晚饭(我已經吃過晚飯)。
我们经过美丽的公园(我們經過美麗的公園)，继续向前走(繼續向前走)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen jīngguò měilì de gōngyuán, jìxù xiàng qián zǒu.
examples.english: We passed through the beautiful park and continued walking forward.

examples.chinese: 他的成绩超过了所有同学(他的成績超過了所有同學)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de chéngjì chāoguòle suǒyǒu tóngxué.
examples.english: His grades exceeded all his classmates.
